item.title: U.S. vows to "swiftly punish" Iran as at least 3 troops killed
item.description: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

item.title: F-16s intercept multiple planes in restricted airspace over New Jersey
item.description: F-16 fighter jets intercepted multiple planes that flew through restricted airspace over Bedminster and East Rutherford, New Jersey, on Sunday.

item.title: Body found at Boston home owned by husband of Rep. Ayanna Pressley
item.description: Boston police are investigating the discovery Saturday of a dead body that was found at a property that is owned by the husband of Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley.

item.title: Video shows arrest of Tate brothers in Miami
item.description: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested in Miami on Saturday after prosecutors in the U.K. announced dozens of new charges against the brothers. The two have denied any wrongdoing. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Taylor Farms lettuce sample was false positive for cyclospora, FDA says
item.description: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing for Cyclospora."

item.title: Andy Burnham takes the reins as U.K.'s 7th prime minister in a decade
item.description: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.

item.title: Sample of Taylor Farms lettuce that tested positive for cyclospora was false positive, FDA says
item.description: The FDA says a sample of Taylor Farms shredded iceberg lettuce that it initially reported positive for the parasite cyclospora was a false positive. The outbreak has sickened more than 1,600 people across five states. Ian Lee reports.

item.title: U.S. soldiers killed in Iranian attack on base in Jordan identified
item.description: The U.S. military has identified two soldiers killed in an Iranian attack on a U.S. base in Jordan: 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas. Charlie D'Agata has the latest.

item.title: Andy Burnham speaks for first time as Britain's new prime minister
item.description: Newly minted British Prime Minister Andy Burnham addressed crowds at 10 Downing Street after a meeting with King Charles III. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab joins to discuss Burnham's speech.

item.title: Pentagon ID's 2 soldiers killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: Spain wins 2026 FIFA World Cup with grueling 1-0 victory over Argentina
item.description: Spain dominated the match, outshooting Argentina 20-3, but couldn't manage a goal in regulation — finally scoring in the second period of extra time.

item.title: Cat pulled from Venezuela quake rubble as death toll tops 5,200
item.description: A weakened cat was pulled from under the rubble of a collapsed residential complex in what a rescuer described as a "ray of hope."

item.title: Vice President JD Vance and wife Usha welcome their 4th child
item.description: This is the first time in more than 150 years that a vice president's family had a child while in office.

item.title: Fire captain, wife, 3 sons found dead after flash flood in Utah
item.description: The victims were a captain in the Provo, Utah, fire department, his wife and their sons, according to fire officials.

item.title: Student loan defaults surging in wake of COVID-tied payments pause expiration
item.description: Defaults on student loans have surged across the United States, reaching record levels as borrowers struggle to keep up with payments.

item.title: Severely ill prisoners granted early release are left stuck behind bars
item.description: Prisoners with severe medical conditions can be granted compassionate release, but without a long-term care facility willing to accept them, many continue to wait in prison as their conditions worsen.

item.title: FBI shifts immigration agent cases to HSI after recent fatal shootings, sources say
item.description: HSI is an arm of ICE, meaning the immigration enforcement agency would effectively be investigating incidents involving its own agents.

item.title: American service member killed in Iraq; U.S. hits Iran for 9th straight night
item.description: Two U.S. service members were killed earlier in Jordan and a third was missing in action, the U.S. Central Command said.

item.title: Spain and Argentina's World Cup trophy count before 2026 final
item.description: Spain and Argentina faced off Sunday in the World Cup final, marking just the second meeting between the two sides in the tournament's history, and their first in 60 years.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.

item.title: Anderson Cooper on witnessing the monarch butterfly migration
item.description: The annual migration of monarch butterflies is one of science's great mysteries: millions of monarchs know the correct path even though they have never made the long journey themselves. As 60 Minutes found, the expedition to watch the migration can be its own challenge.

item.title: 7/19: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the U.S. struck back at Iran overnight, Sen. Mark Warner joins to discuss the latest in the Middle East. Plus, CBS News contributors Chris Krebs and David Becker discuss election security.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak sparks race to remove contaminated lettuce
item.description: A cyclospora outbreak, which has sickened thousands of people nationwide, is believed to have been traced to contaminated lettuce from Mexico supplied by produce giant Taylor Farms. Ian Lee has the latest.

item.title: Target recalls over 200,000 children's sandals due to choking risk
item.description: Target said it received 23 reports of the sandal's pearls falling off the shoe.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: The Uplift: Weaving American history
item.description: We travel to South Boston, Virginia, where they are weaving pieces of American history every day at a patriotic flag factory. Plus, David Begnaud shares three heartwarming stories sent to him by viewers.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
item.description: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell has been linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, the CDC said.

item.title: Boomer wealth transfer will mostly benefit the already rich, report finds
item.description: Trillions of wealth passed down by the baby boomers will likely go to younger Americans who are already rich. Here's how much they'll get.

item.title: U.S. concludes another night of Iran strikes amid battle over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure.
